text: Understand the rules before entering the jobsite.
textPreview: Understand the rules before entering the jobsite.

text: Opening PAL title slide with a clean jobsite training look.
textPreview: Opening PAL title slide with a clean jobsite training look.

alt: Opening PAL title slide with a clean jobsite training look.

text:
Narration: Welcome to PAL Environmental Services orientation. This training introduces the safety expectations, required training records, jobsite rules, and reporting procedures every PAL employee must understand before working on a project.
Suggested visual: Opening PAL title slide with a clean jobsite training look.
PAL safety starts with five expectations.
textPreview: PAL safety starts with five expectations.

text: Show five simple goal words around a supervisor speaking with a crew.
textPreview: Show five simple goal words around a supervisor speaking with a crew.

alt: Show five simple goal words around a supervisor speaking with a crew.

text:
Narration: PAL orientation is built around five goals: protecting employee health and safety, preventing accidents through planning and risk assessment, educating employees, meeting or exceeding safety requirements, and keeping clear communication.
Suggested visual: Show five simple goal words around a supervisor speaking with a crew.
Workers must have the training required for the task.
textPreview: Workers must have the training required for the task.

text: NYC SST or OSHA training when required
textPreview: NYC SST or OSHA training when required

text: Fall prevention and scaffold user training when required
textPreview: Fall prevention and scaffold user training when required

text: Fit test and medical clearance before respirator use
textPreview: Fit test and medical clearance before respirator use

text: Safety coordinator reviewing training records with a worker.
textPreview: Safety coordinator reviewing training records with a worker.

text: NYC SST or OSHA training when required
textPreview: NYC SST or OSHA training when required

text: Fall prevention and scaffold user training when required
textPreview: Fall prevention and scaffold user training when required

text: Fit test and medical clearance before respirator use
textPreview: Fit test and medical clearance before respirator use

alt: Safety coordinator reviewing training records with a worker.

text:
Narration: Before working on a PAL project, employees must have the required training records for the work they will perform. Watching this video does not replace those credentials.
Suggested visual: Safety coordinator reviewing training records with a worker.
Minimum PPE is required before work starts.
textPreview: Minimum PPE is required before work starts.

text: Hard hat, safety glasses, work boots, and long pants
textPreview: Hard hat, safety glasses, work boots, and long pants

text: High-visibility vest or clothing when required
textPreview: High-visibility vest or clothing when required

text: Rated gloves matched to the task
textPreview: Rated gloves matched to the task

text: Crew at site entry wearing proper PPE while a supervisor performs a check.
textPreview: Crew at site entry wearing proper PPE while a supervisor performs a check.

alt: Crew at site entry wearing proper PPE while a supervisor performs a check.

text:
Narration: PAL employees must report to the job prepared with required personal protective equipment. Minimum PPE includes approved head, eye, hand, foot, leg, and visibility protection as required by the site and task.
Suggested visual: Crew at site entry wearing proper PPE while a supervisor performs a check.
Face shields, hearing protection, and coveralls may be required
textPreview: Face shields, hearing protection, and coveralls may be required

text: Rubber gloves or boots may be required for specific hazards
textPreview: Rubber gloves or boots may be required for specific hazards

text: Respirators require medical clearance, fit test, training, and authorization
textPreview: Respirators require medical clearance, fit test, training, and authorization

text: Show task-specific PPE neatly arranged, with respirator stored properly.
textPreview: Show task-specific PPE neatly arranged, with respirator stored properly.

text: Face shields, hearing protection, and coveralls may be required
textPreview: Face shields, hearing protection, and coveralls may be required

text: Rubber gloves or boots may be required for specific hazards
textPreview: Rubber gloves or boots may be required for specific hazards

text: Respirators require medical clearance, fit test, training, and authorization
textPreview: Respirators require medical clearance, fit test, training, and authorization

alt: Show task-specific PPE neatly arranged, with respirator stored properly.

text:
Narration: Depending on the work, additional protection may include a face shield, hearing protection, coveralls, rubber gloves or boots, and respiratory protection.
Suggested visual: Show task-specific PPE neatly arranged, with respirator stored properly.
Respirators cannot be used without authorization.
textPreview: Respirators cannot be used without authorization.

text: Respirator authorization checklist. Do not show casual respirator use.
textPreview: Respirator authorization checklist. Do not show casual respirator use.

alt: Respirator authorization checklist. Do not show casual respirator use.

text:
Narration: Do not use a respirator until you have received the required medical clearance, annual fit test, training, and authorization.
Suggested visual: Respirator authorization checklist. Do not show casual respirator use.
Fall hazards begin at six feet or greater.
textPreview: Fall hazards begin at six feet or greater.

text: Open edges, lifts, scaffolds, and walkway gaps
textPreview: Open edges, lifts, scaffolds, and walkway gaps

text: Guardrails and hole covers must be installed and secured
textPreview: Guardrails and hole covers must be installed and secured

text: Barricade below when overhead work creates exposure
textPreview: Barricade below when overhead work creates exposure

text: Guarded edge, secured hole cover, and barricaded work below.
textPreview: Guarded edge, secured hole cover, and barricaded work below.

text: Open edges, lifts, scaffolds, and walkway gaps
textPreview: Open edges, lifts, scaffolds, and walkway gaps

text: Guardrails and hole covers must be installed and secured
textPreview: Guardrails and hole covers must be installed and secured

text: Barricade below when overhead work creates exposure
textPreview: Barricade below when overhead work creates exposure

alt: Guarded edge, secured hole cover, and barricaded work below.

text:
Narration: Fall protection is required when an employee is exposed to a fall hazard of six feet or greater.
Suggested visual: Guarded edge, secured hole cover, and barricaded work below.
Inspect fall protection before every use.
textPreview: Inspect fall protection before every use.

text: Check harness, lanyard, connectors, and anchor point
textPreview: Check harness, lanyard, connectors, and anchor point

text: Stop work if the setup is unclear or defective
textPreview: Stop work if the setup is unclear or defective

text: Worker inspecting harness and tying off to an approved point in a lift.
textPreview: Worker inspecting harness and tying off to an approved point in a lift.

text: Check harness, lanyard, connectors, and anchor point
textPreview: Check harness, lanyard, connectors, and anchor point

text: Stop work if the setup is unclear or defective
textPreview: Stop work if the setup is unclear or defective

alt: Worker inspecting harness and tying off to an approved point in a lift.

text:
Narration: Personal fall arrest equipment must be inspected before every use, and anchor points must meet the required rating.
Suggested visual: Worker inspecting harness and tying off to an approved point in a lift.
Portable ladders are the last option.
textPreview: Portable ladders are the last option.

text: 4. Traditional portable ladder only when permitted
textPreview: 4. Traditional portable ladder only when permitted

text: Show the PAL Ladder Last hierarchy clearly from safer option to last resort.
textPreview: Show the PAL Ladder Last hierarchy clearly from safer option to last resort.

text: Traditional portable ladder only when permitted
textPreview: Traditional portable ladder only when permitted

alt: Show the PAL Ladder Last hierarchy clearly from safer option to last resort.

text:
Narration: PAL takes its Ladders Last Policy seriously. Traditional portable ladders must not be the primary work platform when a safer option is feasible.
Suggested visual: Show the PAL Ladder Last hierarchy clearly from safer option to last resort.
Select access equipment before the task starts.
textPreview: Select access equipment before the task starts.

text: Supervisors choose the safest feasible platform
textPreview: Supervisors choose the safest feasible platform

text: Do not overreach or work outside side rails
textPreview: Do not overreach or work outside side rails

text: Stop if the selected equipment is not safe for the work
textPreview: Stop if the selected equipment is not safe for the work

text: Supervisor and worker choosing access equipment during pre-task planning.
textPreview: Supervisor and worker choosing access equipment during pre-task planning.

text: Supervisors choose the safest feasible platform
textPreview: Supervisors choose the safest feasible platform

text: Do not overreach or work outside side rails
textPreview: Do not overreach or work outside side rails

text: Stop if the selected equipment is not safe for the work
textPreview: Stop if the selected equipment is not safe for the work

alt: Supervisor and worker choosing access equipment during pre-task planning.

text:
Narration: Supervisors must select the proper access equipment during pre-task planning. If the selected equipment does not allow the work to be performed safely, stop and notify supervision.
Suggested visual: Supervisor and worker choosing access equipment during pre-task planning.
Inspect cords and protect against shock.
textPreview: Inspect cords and protect against shock.

text: Remove damaged electrical equipment from service
textPreview: Remove damaged electrical equipment from service

text: Worker inspecting cord and testing GFCI; damaged cord tagged out.
textPreview: Worker inspecting cord and testing GFCI; damaged cord tagged out.

text: Remove damaged electrical equipment from service
textPreview: Remove damaged electrical equipment from service

alt: Worker inspecting cord and testing GFCI; damaged cord tagged out.

text:
Narration: Inspect extension cords, plugs, temporary wiring, and ground-fault circuit interrupters before use.
Suggested visual: Worker inspecting cord and testing GFCI; damaged cord tagged out.
Use tools only when trained and protected.
textPreview: Use tools only when trained and protected.

text: Inspect tools, guards, cords, and accessories
textPreview: Inspect tools, guards, cords, and accessories

text: Tool inspection beside lock and tag applied to isolated equipment.
textPreview: Tool inspection beside lock and tag applied to isolated equipment.

text: Inspect tools, guards, cords, and accessories
textPreview: Inspect tools, guards, cords, and accessories

alt: Tool inspection beside lock and tag applied to isolated equipment.

text:
Narration: Only trained employees may operate hand and power tools. Hazardous energy must be properly de-energized, verified, locked, and tagged by authorized personnel.
Suggested visual: Tool inspection beside lock and tag applied to isolated equipment.
Plan the lift before touching the load.
textPreview: Plan the lift before touching the load.

text: Know the weight, size, and shape
textPreview: Know the weight, size, and shape

text: Use pallet jacks, dollies, hoists, forklifts, or team lifting when practical
textPreview: Use pallet jacks, dollies, hoists, forklifts, or team lifting when practical

text: Stop and get help if the load is too heavy or awkward
textPreview: Stop and get help if the load is too heavy or awkward

text: Crew planning a lift and using a pallet jack.
textPreview: Crew planning a lift and using a pallet jack.

text: Know the weight, size, and shape
textPreview: Know the weight, size, and shape

text: Use pallet jacks, dollies, hoists, forklifts, or team lifting when practical
textPreview: Use pallet jacks, dollies, hoists, forklifts, or team lifting when practical

text: Stop and get help if the load is too heavy or awkward
textPreview: Stop and get help if the load is too heavy or awkward

alt: Crew planning a lift and using a pallet jack.

text:
Narration: Plan every lift. Know the load's weight, size, and shape. Use mechanical help when practical.
Suggested visual: Crew planning a lift and using a pallet jack.
Keep the load close and avoid twisting.
textPreview: Keep the load close and avoid twisting.

text: Secure material so it cannot shift
textPreview: Secure material so it cannot shift

text: Worker lifting bags from a pallet jack with proper body position.
textPreview: Worker lifting bags from a pallet jack with proper body position.

text: Secure material so it cannot shift
textPreview: Secure material so it cannot shift

alt: Worker lifting bags from a pallet jack with proper body position.

text:
Narration: Keep the load close, lift with your legs, avoid twisting, secure material against movement, and maintain clear communication.
Suggested visual: Worker lifting bags from a pallet jack with proper body position.
Control ignition sources and keep an exit route.
textPreview: Control ignition sources and keep an exit route.

text: Hot work requires an approved permit and controls
textPreview: Hot work requires an approved permit and controls

text: Call 911 and evacuate if the fire is too large
textPreview: Call 911 and evacuate if the fire is too large

text: Use PASS only if trained and conditions allow
textPreview: Use PASS only if trained and conditions allow

text: Hot-work controls, clear extinguisher access, and safe exit path.
textPreview: Hot-work controls, clear extinguisher access, and safe exit path.

text: Hot work requires an approved permit and controls
textPreview: Hot work requires an approved permit and controls

text: Call 911 and evacuate if the fire is too large
textPreview: Call 911 and evacuate if the fire is too large

text: Use PASS only if trained and conditions allow
textPreview: Use PASS only if trained and conditions allow

alt: Hot-work controls, clear extinguisher access, and safe exit path.

text:
Narration: Fire prevention means controlling ignition sources and combustible materials. If a fire is too large to control safely, call 911 and evacuate.
Suggested visual: Hot-work controls, clear extinguisher access, and safe exit path.
Keep walkways, stairs, doors, and emergency equipment clear
textPreview: Keep walkways, stairs, doors, and emergency equipment clear

text: Clean spills or isolate the area
textPreview: Clean spills or isolate the area

text: Store materials so they cannot fall, shift, or block access
textPreview: Store materials so they cannot fall, shift, or block access

text: Crew member cleaning debris and keeping the pathway clear.
textPreview: Crew member cleaning debris and keeping the pathway clear.

text: Keep walkways, stairs, doors, and emergency equipment clear
textPreview: Keep walkways, stairs, doors, and emergency equipment clear

text: Clean spills or isolate the area
textPreview: Clean spills or isolate the area

text: Store materials so they cannot fall, shift, or block access
textPreview: Store materials so they cannot fall, shift, or block access

alt: Crew member cleaning debris and keeping the pathway clear.

text:
Narration: Good housekeeping is required every day. Keep access clear, clean spills immediately, and store materials so they do not create hazards.
Suggested visual: Crew member cleaning debris and keeping the pathway clear.
Know where SDS and Right-to-Know information are located
textPreview: Know where SDS and Right-to-Know information are located

text: Isolate unlabeled material and notify supervision
textPreview: Isolate unlabeled material and notify supervision

text: Worker checking a labeled container against an SDS binder or tablet.
textPreview: Worker checking a labeled container against an SDS binder or tablet.

text: Know where SDS and Right-to-Know information are located
textPreview: Know where SDS and Right-to-Know information are located

text: Isolate unlabeled material and notify supervision
textPreview: Isolate unlabeled material and notify supervision

alt: Worker checking a labeled container against an SDS binder or tablet.

text:
Narration: Hazard Communication and GHS help employees understand chemical hazards. Never use an unknown or unlabeled material.
Suggested visual: Worker checking a labeled container against an SDS binder or tablet.
Erected and inspected under competent-person control
textPreview: Erected and inspected under competent-person control

text: Never stand, sit, or rest on guardrails
textPreview: Never stand, sit, or rest on guardrails

text: Competent person reviewing scaffold tag and checklist.
textPreview: Competent person reviewing scaffold tag and checklist.

text: Erected and inspected under competent-person control
textPreview: Erected and inspected under competent-person control

text: Never stand, sit, or rest on guardrails
textPreview: Never stand, sit, or rest on guardrails

alt: Competent person reviewing scaffold tag and checklist.

text:
Narration: Scaffolds must be erected by a competent person, installed according to manufacturer requirements, and inspected and documented daily.
Suggested visual: Competent person reviewing scaffold tag and checklist.
Only trained employees may operate lifts.
textPreview: Only trained employees may operate lifts.

text: Conduct and document the pre-use inspection
textPreview: Conduct and document the pre-use inspection

text: Use approved tie-off points when required
textPreview: Use approved tie-off points when required

text: Keep both feet on the platform floor
textPreview: Keep both feet on the platform floor

text: Scissor lift inspection and worker tied off to designated point.
textPreview: Scissor lift inspection and worker tied off to designated point.

textPreview: Conduct and document the pre-use inspection

textPreview: Keep both feet on the platform floor

text:
Narration: Only trained and authorized employees may operate mobile elevating work platforms or scissor lifts.
Suggested visual: Scissor lift inspection and worker tied off to designated point.
Stay visible and out of the equipment path.
textPreview: Stay visible and out of the equipment path.

text: Stay out of blind spots and loading areas
textPreview: Stay out of blind spots and loading areas

text: Make eye contact with the operator before approaching

This orientation does not authorize entry.

textPreview: Hazards may include atmosphere, engulfment, or entrapment

textPreview: Do not enter unless trained, authorized, and equipped

Suggested visual: Permit-required confined-space warning with untrained worker stopped outside.
Only trained authorized personnel may disturb asbestos or lead
textPreview: Only trained authorized personnel may disturb asbestos or lead

text: Follow the task-specific silica exposure control plan
textPreview: Follow the task-specific silica exposure control plan

text: Use wet methods, HEPA vacuums, dust collection, and PPE as required

textPreview: Controlled regulated area with HEPA vacuum and wet dust control.

text: Only trained authorized personnel may disturb asbestos or lead

textPreview: Use wet methods, HEPA vacuums, dust collection, and PPE as required

alt: Controlled regulated area with HEPA vacuum and wet dust control.

text:
Narration: Only authorized and trained personnel may disturb asbestos-containing material or lead. Respirable crystalline silica can cause serious illness and must be controlled.
Suggested visual: Controlled regulated area with HEPA vacuum and wet dust control.
Regulated waste does not go in the regular dumpster.
textPreview: Regulated waste does not go in the regular dumpster.

text: Use labeled waste streams and closed containers
textPreview: Use labeled waste streams and closed containers

text: Keep spill kits where waste is generated
textPreview: Keep spill kits where waste is generated

text: Ask supervision before disposal when uncertain
textPreview: Ask supervision before disposal when uncertain

text: Labeled waste containers, spill kit, and supervisor directing disposal.

textPreview: Use labeled waste streams and closed containers

text: Keep spill kits where waste is generated

text: Ask supervision before disposal when uncertain
textPreview: Ask supervision before disposal when uncertain

alt: Labeled waste containers, spill kit, and supervisor directing disposal.

text:
Narration: Batteries, fluorescent lamps, chemicals, aerosol cans, used oil, and other regulated materials must be handled and disposed of correctly.
Suggested visual: Labeled waste containers, spill kit, and supervisor directing disposal.
Know the plan before work begins.
textPreview: Know the plan before work begins.

text: Know alarms, emergency contacts, route, and muster point
textPreview: Know alarms, emergency contacts, route, and muster point

text: Remain at the muster point until released
textPreview: Remain at the muster point until released

text: Workers calmly walking to a muster point while supervisor takes headcount.

text:
Narration: Every project must post a site-specific Emergency Action Plan. Before beginning work, know the alarms, emergency contacts, evacuation route, and muster point.
Suggested visual: Workers calmly walking to a muster point while supervisor takes headcount.
Report incidents and near misses immediately.
textPreview: Report incidents and near misses immediately.

text: Injuries, illnesses, property damage, releases, and near misses
textPreview: Injuries, illnesses, property damage, releases, and near misses

text: Report even when it appears minor
textPreview: Report even when it appears minor

text: Same-day review corrects hazards and prevents repeat events
textPreview: Same-day review corrects hazards and prevents repeat events

text: Worker calmly reporting a near miss to supervisor.
textPreview: Worker calmly reporting a near miss to supervisor.

text: Injuries, illnesses, property damage, releases, and near misses
textPreview: Injuries, illnesses, property damage, releases, and near misses

text: Report even when it appears minor
textPreview: Report even when it appears minor

text: Same-day review corrects hazards and prevents repeat events
textPreview: Same-day review corrects hazards and prevents repeat events

text:
Narration: All accidents, injuries, illnesses, property damage, environmental releases, and near misses must be reported immediately.
Suggested visual: Worker calmly reporting a near miss to supervisor.
No one has the right to put you in an unsafe situation.
textPreview: No one has the right to put you in an unsafe situation.

text: Worker stopping a task and speaking with foreman before continuing.
textPreview: Worker stopping a task and speaking with foreman before continuing.

alt: Worker stopping a task and speaking with foreman before continuing.

text:
Narration: Employees will not be penalized for refusing work they reasonably believe is unsafe. Stop the work, move to a safe location, and notify supervision.
Suggested visual: Worker stopping a task and speaking with foreman before continuing.
PAL maintains a drug-free and alcohol-free workplace.
textPreview: PAL maintains a drug-free and alcohol-free workplace.

textPreview: No alcohol during the workday, including lunch

Narration: PAL maintains a drug-free and alcohol-free workplace. Employees and applicants must comply with PAL testing policy.

When you are unsure, stop and ask.

Suggested visual: Foreman answering worker questions during a jobsite meeting.
